terms.definition: Choosing a subset of original input features for modeling while discarding the rest, reducing dimensionality without creating new combined features.
Feature scoring that ranks or tests each feature using a statistic computed from data, largely independent of a specific learning algorithm.
Subset search that repeatedly trains and evaluates a model on candidate feature sets, treating the learner as part of the selection loop.
Feature selection performed during model training via penalties or internal importance mechanisms (e.g. L1 sparsity, tree impurity).
A wrapper that iteratively fits a model, removes the least important features, and refits until a target count remains.
An information-theoretic filter statistic measuring how much a feature reduces uncertainty about the target; captures non-linear dependence.
